description: capital of the Colombian department of Valle del Cauca
description_wiki: Santiago de Cali (Spanish pronunciation: [sanˈtjaɣo ðe ˈkali]), or Cali, is the capital of the Valle del Cauca department, and the most populous city in southwest Colombia, with an estimated 2,280,522 residents in 2023.
Colombia, officially the Republic of Colombia, is a country located in South America, with insular regions in North America.

description: Colombian private higher education institution founded in 1930, with its main facilities in Bogotá and a second campus in Cali
description_wiki:
The Pontificia Universidad Javeriana (English:
Xavierian Pontifical University) is a private university in Colombia founded in 1623.

The term Universidad del Valle could refer to the following:

Universidad del Valle (Colombia), one of the biggest and most important universities in Colombia.

description: public research university in Minneapolis and Saint Paul, Minnesota, United States
description_wiki: The University of Minnesota Twin Cities (historically known as University of Minnesota) is a public land-grant research university in the Twin Cities of Minneapolis and Saint Paul, Minnesota, United States.

description: postgraduate academic degree awarded by most universities worldwide
description_wiki: A Doctor of Philosophy (PhD, DPhil; Latin: philosophiae doctor or doctor in philosophia) is a terminal degree that usually denotes the highest level of academic achievement in a given discipline and is awarded following a course of graduate study and original research.
